compiled_state.title: Replace SIGPIPE-vulnerable echo|grep -q patterns in batch-1 test files
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
Replace all echo|grep -q patterns with bash-native equivalents in the first 26 test files (alphabetically). Use the flag-to-bash mapping from the parent epic:
- grep -q PATTERN → [[ "$var" =~ PATTERN ]]
- grep -qi PATTERN → shopt -s nocasematch; [[ "$var" =~ PATTERN ]]; shopt -u nocasematch
- grep -qF STRING → [[ "$var" == *"STRING"* ]]
- grep -qE PATTERN → [[ "$var" =~ PATTERN ]]
- Negated: ! grep -q → ! [[ "$var" =~ PATTERN ]]

Capture the echo output to a variable first, then use [[ ]] on the variable.

## TDD Exemption
Exempt — mechanical string replacement with no conditional logic change. The existing test suite IS the verification (criterion 1: no conditional logic, criterion 2: tests verify unchanged behavior). Run all modified test files after replacement to confirm no regressions.

## Files to Modify
Run: grep -rl 'echo.*|.*grep -q' tests/ | head -26 (alphabetically sorted) to find affected files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero echo|grep -q patterns in batch-1 files
  Verify: count=$(grep -rl 'echo.*|.*grep -q' tests/ | sort | head -26 | xargs grep -l 'echo.*|.*grep -q' 2>/dev/null | wc -l); test "$count" -eq 0
- [ ] All modified test files pass
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh 2>&1 | tail -5
- [ ] No syntax errors in modified files
  Verify: for f in $(git diff --name-only); do bash -n "$f" || exit 1; done

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Replacements complete — zero echo|grep -q patterns remain across all 26 files ✓
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: All 26 modified test files pass (26/26) — zero failures ✓
